summary: Betty‑board soundboard delivers crystal‑clear mix while the set shines with a monster Eyes of the World and a smoking Slipknot! despite occasional track‑break frustrations.
review: The recording benefits from Betty’s meticulous board work, giving every instrument – especially Jerry’s clear lead tones and Phil’s rare bass solo – excellent definition. The performance is highlighted by an electrifying Eyes of the World jam that flows into a standout Slipknot!, plus tight renditions of Around And Around and Dancing in the Street. A few listeners complain about excessive track breaks, which detracts from an otherwise superb listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Four out of five reviews rave about both the musical performance and the high‑quality Betty‑board mix; only one review is sharply negative, focusing on track‑break issues rather than the music itself.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Betty’s board provides a balanced, crisp sound with clear Jerry guitar and audible bass solo. The only drawback mentioned is the profusion of track breaks that interrupt playback.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set moves smoothly from a tight first half (despite an early technical hiccup) into a second half that builds momentum, highlighted by extended jams and seamless transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His tone is described as clear, cool and delectable; notable use of a Moog synth and the new Mutron envelope filter.
band_member_comments.Phil: Delivers a rare, distinct bass solo during Eyes of the World – praised as amazing.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned briefly having technical difficulties early in the first set, but no specific performance critique.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith appears only fleetingly on an electric keyboard/Moog; reviewers note his brief appearance and later skepticism toward synths.
